name: Turkey Chili
recipeIngredient: 28 ounces whole plum tomatoes, drained and chopped
2 chipotle chilies in adobo sauce, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven and sauté the onions until golden.
2. Add the garlic and sauté for another 2-3 minutes.
3. Add the turkey and continue to sauté until the meat loses its color, stirring to break apart.
4. Sprinkle the masa harina over the meat and stir to incorporate, cooking for an additional 2 minutes.
5. Add the remaining ingredients (except beans), the reserved tomato juice, and 2 cups of chicken stock. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, stirring occasionally for 1.5 hours, adding more chicken stock if necessary.
6. Add the beans and continue cooking for an additional 10-15 minutes until heated through. Serve with cornbread, tortillas, diced onions, grated cheese, sour cream, chopped fresh cilantro, or any other accompaniments you prefer.
Note: Try to use the ingredients listed. If regular chili powder is used, be aware that it may contain a mixture of chili powder, cumin, salt, and other spices. This recipe strongly suggests using the listed ingredients for the best flavor. The chili has an earthy taste with a hint of smokiness from the chipotle chilies in adobo sauce. Beef or pork can substitute for the turkey.
